questions.q: Rosa Parks's 1955 refusal to give up her seat on a city bus sparked a year-long boycott in which Alabama city?
Who became the first African-American President of the United States, elected in 2008?
Which abolitionist made about 13 trips on the Underground Railroad, guiding dozens of enslaved people to freedom?
The Emancipation Proclamation, issued by which president in 1863, declared enslaved people in Confederate states free?
Juneteenth — now a federal holiday — commemorates what 1865 event?
choices: News of emancipation reaching Galveston, Texas

Who was the first African American to win the Academy Award for Best Actor?
Who was the first African American woman elected to the U.S. Congress?
Who was the influential civil rights activist and leader of the Nation of Islam known for advocating Black nationalism?

Who was the first African American to hold the position of U.S. Secretary of State?
What was the primary goal of the Freedom Rides that began in 1961?
choices: To challenge segregation in public schools

questions.q: Who was the first African American to be awarded the Nobel Prize in Literature?
What was the primary goal of the Underground Railroad during the 19th century?
choices: To build transportation infrastructure in the South
To establish mining operations in Northern states
To create a network of railroad companies
To help enslaved people escape to free states and Canada

questions.q: Which Supreme Court decision in 1954 declared that 'separate but equal' schools were unconstitutional?

Which author wrote the novel 'Their Eyes Were Watching God,' published in 1937?

choices: To create political alliances with European nations
To build factories in New York
To establish a period of African American cultural, artistic, and intellectual flourishing
To develop new forms of agriculture

questions.q: Which African American female athlete was the first Black woman to win a gold medal at the Olympic Games in 1948?

In what year did the Emancipation Proclamation take effect, freeing enslaved people in Confederate states?
Who was the African American singer and actress who won an Academy Award for her role in 'Gone with the Wind' in 1940?

In what year did Martin Luther King Jr. deliver his famous 'I Have a Dream' speech?

Jazz music originated in which American city in the early 20th century?

choices: Secure voting rights in the South
Achieve civil rights and economic equality

questions.q: Who was the first African American mayor of a major U.S. city, elected in Cleveland, Ohio in 1967?

What style of music, born from African American communities, blends blues, ragtime, and improvisation?

Which historian, known as the 'Father of Black History,' launched Negro History Week in 1926?
In 1989, which Army general became the first African-American to chair the Joint Chiefs of Staff?

Which African American sociologist and historian founded the Association for the Study of African American Life and History in 1915?

choices: Civil rights attorney and NAACP Legal Defense Fund director

questions.q: Which African American inventor patented the three-position traffic light in 1923?
What pseudonym did Malcolm X use when he first joined the Nation of Islam in 1952?

choices: Huey P. Newton and Bobby Seale
Eldridge Cleaver and H. Rap Brown

questions.q: What was the primary focus of the National Association of Colored Women (NACW), founded by Mary Church Terrell in 1896?
choices: Social welfare, education, and racial uplift through community service
Labor union organization and workplace rights

questions.q: Which African American playwright wrote 'A Raisin in the Sun,' which premiered on Broadway in 1959?
What was the name of the legal case that resulted in the invalidation of 'separate but equal' doctrine in higher education in 1950?
choices: Missouri ex rel. Gaines v. Canada

questions.q: Who was the African American economist and first president of the Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta?

choices: Integration of African American artists into mainstream galleries
Creating art that promoted Black consciousness and independence from white cultural standards
Reviving traditional African art forms exclusively

questions.q: Who was the first African American to win the Pulitzer Prize for Fiction, awarded in 1983?
Which historically Black college was founded in 1867 and is known for producing Howard University School of Medicine?

Which African American mathematician and physicist made significant contributions to the Manhattan Project during World War II?
What was the primary focus of the Tuskegee Airmen during World War II?
choices: Military fighter pilots and aerial combat

questions.q: What underground railroad station's operation in Philadelphia was documented in the records of William Still?

What was the primary goal of the Black Panther Party when it was founded in Oakland, California in 1966?
choices: To promote separatist ideology through educational institutions
To organize a nationwide voter registration campaign
To establish an independent Black nation in the United States
To secure armed self-defense and community patrols against police brutality

questions.q: Which African American abolitionist and orator escaped slavery and later met with European royalty during his speaking tours?

What was the primary legislation passed by the Reconstruction Congress that granted citizenship to formerly enslaved people?

choices: The North Carolina Civil Rights Sit-In

questions.q: What was the primary mission of the Freedmen's Bureau established after the Civil War?
choices: To provide assistance and education to formerly enslaved people and war refugees
To enforce segregation laws in Southern states
To manage plantations abandoned by Confederate soldiers
To organize Black voter suppression prevention

questions.q: Which African American historian wrote the groundbreaking work 'Black Reconstruction in America' examining the Reconstruction period?
What organization did Marcus Garvey found in 1914 to promote African American nationalism and pan-Africanism?
choices: The National Association for the Advancement of Colored People

questions.q: Which African American athlete became the first to break the color barrier in professional football in 1946?
Which civil rights activist was the primary founder of the Nation of Islam's Temple Number 7 in New York City?

choices: Self-determination, self-defense, and economic independence for Black communities

questions.q: Who was the first African American to earn a Ph.D. in physics, receiving it from the University of Michigan in 1933?

Who was the African American jurist appointed as the first Black Federal District Court Judge in 1966?
Which African American inventor patented the three-position railroad switch and signal in 1887?
Who founded the Journal of Negro History in 1916, establishing a scholarly foundation for Black historical research?

Who was the pioneering African American neurosurgeon who developed techniques for operating on the brain and spinal cord?
What was the primary focus of the Niagara Movement, founded in 1905 by W.E.B. Du Bois and other African American intellectuals?
choices: Demanding full civil rights and opposing Booker T. Washington's accommodationism
Organizing labor unions for Black workers

questions.q: Which African American author wrote 'Invisible Man,' published in 1952, exploring themes of identity and racial invisibility?

choices: Rosa Parks' refusal to give up her seat to a white passenger
A bus driver's racial slur against a passenger
A dispute over bus fare pricing

questions.q: Thurgood Marshall became the first African American Supreme Court Justice in which year?

choices: In the Heat of the Night

questions.q: Shirley Chisholm became the first African American woman elected to the U.S. House of Representatives in what year?

To escape slavery in the South

questions.q: Which pioneering African American aviator earned the nickname 'The Bronze Eagle' during her flying career in the 1920s?

choices: March on Washington for Jobs and Freedom

questions.q: Which African American poet and activist was a central figure of the Black Arts Movement of the 1960s and 1970s?

choices: The start of the March on Washington

questions.q: In 1921, what was the name of the prosperous African American community in Tulsa, Oklahoma that was destroyed during a racial massacre?

choices: School desegregation and the unconstitutionality of separate but equal doctrine
